name: The Cascade Witness
description: Scholar Daxis has been working from instrument data for three years. She's never spoken to someone who was actually at the control panel when the Cascade happened. The Keeper has been in the control chamber for three hundred years.
accept_text: She looks at you the way researchers look when they've just had a door open that they didn't know existed. 'Three hundred years.' She says it quietly. 'The control chamber. There is someone in the control chamber who was there when it happened.' She is quiet for a moment. 'I have spent three years working from the scar data — the emission pattern, the resonance trace, the reconstruction of the wave. I've been working backward from the evidence to the event.' She pauses. 'There is someone who was at the panel when the instruments went dark. Who counted forty seconds.' She looks at the Resonance Core. 'I know it from the scar. They know it because they were here.' She gives you a notebook page, filled with her handwriting. 'Take this. It's the twelve questions I have been unable to answer from the instrument data alone. Ask them what they saw. Get the sequence, in order, as precisely as they can recall it.' She pauses. 'And tell them — they said they counted the forty seconds while the nodes went dark. Ask them which node went last.' She looks at the Core. 'I have a hypothesis about the propagation sequence. If I'm right about which node went last, it changes my estimate of where the inversion originated.' She meets your eyes. 'I've been working the wrong end of the wave. If the inversion origin was below the Silent Market — which is what the data suggests — I need their confirmation of the propagation sequence to prove it.' She pauses. 'Ask them everything on that list. I'll be here when you come back.'
complete_text: She reads through your notes standing up, cross-referencing against the notebook in her other hand. You watch her expression change three times — the first when she reaches the propagation sequence, the second when she reaches the node order, the third when she reaches the detail about forty seconds. She closes her notebook. 'The last node,' she says. 'South-west of the core, below grade. Below the surface.' She looks at the Resonance Core. 'That is the Silent Market's foundation level. The inversion propagated from below the Silent Market.' She is quiet for a moment. 'Which means whoever triggered the Cascade was not on the surface. They were underground. Beneath the busiest market in the pre-war city.' She looks at the scar. 'The Silent Market is silent now because of the Cascade. The Cascade originated from beneath it.' She pauses. 'That is not an accident of geography. Whoever triggered the inversion chose a location beneath a population center.' She sets the notebook down. 'I need to go speak to the Keeper directly. I have twelve more questions.' She looks at you. 'You've been a very good carrier of information. But I think I need to have this conversation myself.'
